core_mechanism: symmetry-breaking bifurcation of a spatially-distributed system, in which a spatially uniform (or single-peaked) steady state loses stability as a control parameter crosses a critical 'sustain' point and gives way to a new patterned steady state (a paired/twin concentration)
brief_reason: The abstract frames the emergence of twin agglomerations around a central place as a bifurcation of an evenly-distributed spatial configuration at a sustain point, i.e. a critical parameter value where the symmetric state becomes unstable and a patterned state branches off.
